description_html:
Game Jam

  • The Game Jam is a 48-hour offline game development challenge, conducted as a flagship event of the college fest by the Game Development Community.
  • This event encourages in-person collaboration, creativity, and rapid prototyping among participants.
  • The Game Jam Theme will be revealed at starting of the game jam in college.
  • The Savar Fest Pass is required to pariticpate the event.

Guidelines:

General Requirements:

Theme Compliance:

  • The game must be clearly related to the announced theme.
  • Creative interpretations are allowed; complete deviation may affect judging.

Playability:

  • The submitted game must be playable from start to finish.
  • Game-breaking bugs may negatively impact evaluation.

Content Restrictions

  • Games must NOT include:
  • NSFW content
  • Offensive material
  • Discriminatory or hateful content
  • The intent is to ensure games are playable by everyone.

Registration:

  • Once the team has registered at the college, they are free to build the game anywhere.
  • All team members must be present during registration at the college.
  • Directly submitting a game to itch.io without college registration will result in disqualification.
  • Violation of these rules may lead to disqualification.

Platform & Compatibility Requirements:

Target Platform:

  • The game must be runnable on PC (Windows).
  • Submissions must include a Windows-compatible build or executable.

Prohibited Platforms:

  • VR-based games are not allowed.

Accessibility:

  • Games should be playable using commonly available input devices.
  • Browser-playable games are encouraged over downloadable builds.

Tools:

  • Participants may use any game engine, framework, or technology.
  • No restriction on programming languages.

Assets & Resources:

Allowed Assets:

  • Self-created assets
  • Third-party assets, provided:
  • Proper licensing is followed
  • Credits are given where applicable

Asset Creation

  • Creating everything from scratch is not mandatory.
  • Teams choosing to do so are encouraged and appreciated.

Rulebook :

Team Rules:

Participation Format:

  • Solo and team participation are allowed.
  • Team participation is encouraged.
  • A participant CANNOT be part of more than one team.
  • Participants registered in multiple teams or both solo and team formats will be disqualified.

Team Size:

  • Maximum team size: 5 members
  • Larger teams may face coordination challenges within the limited time.

 Accomodation:

  • Participants are responsible for arranging their own lodging, as the college will not be providing accommodation for the game jam

Submission Rules:

Submission Platform:

  • All submissions must be uploaded directly to itch.io.
  • Linking to external websites or download sources will result in disqualification.

Submission Integrity:

  • Once submitted, deleted submissions will be ineligible for judging.
  • Each submission must include:
  • A playable Windows build
  • Game description
  • Controls and instructions
  • Credits (if applicable)

Deadline & Grace Policy:

  • No grace period will be provided.
  • Submissions must be completed before the official deadline.
  • Participants are advised to allow sufficient time for uploading and verification.

Judging & Evaluation:

  • Final results will be calculated using an 80–20 evaluation system:

Judges’ Evaluation — 80%

  • Games will be evaluated on:
  • Theme interpretation
  • Gameplay & mechanics
  • Creativity & originality
  • Technical implementation
  • Overall polish
  • Game Dev Community Rating — 20%
  • Members of the Game Development Community will rate the games.
  • In case of ties or disputes, the judges’ decision will be final.
  • Disqualification Conditions:
  • A team or participant may be disqualified for:
  • Violating content guidelines
  • Submitting VR-based games
  • Missing the submission deadline
  • Deleting the itch.io submission
  • Providing external download links
  • Multiple registrations by the same participant
  • Submitting a game without college registration
  • Multiple submissions of the same game (both teams will be disqualified)
